edges.mechanism: IAA binds TIR1 F-box receptor and Aux/IAA forming co-receptor complex
edges.evidence_sentence: TIR1/AFB Aux/IAA nuclear auxin perception triggers local activation of H+-ATPases

edges.mechanism: SCF(TIR1) E3 ligase polyubiquitinates Aux/IAA proteins for proteasomal degradation in presence of auxin
edges.evidence_sentence: auxin-induced polyubiquitination and proteasomal degradation of Aux/IAA

edges.mechanism: AFB1 (TIR1 family) recruits Aux/IAA for degradation
edges.evidence_sentence: TIR1/AFB receptors degrade Aux/IAA proteins

edges.mechanism: AFB2 receptor recruits Aux/IAAs
edges.evidence_sentence: TIR1/AFB receptors degrade Aux/IAA proteins

edges.mechanism: AFB5 has distinct ligand specificity, prominent in hypocotyl
edges.evidence_sentence: afb5-5 leads to decreased sensitivity to picloram-induced hypocotyl elongation

edges.mechanism: Aux/IAA proteins bind ARF6 forming repressive heterodimer
edges.evidence_sentence: Aux/IAA proteins interact with ARF proteins to repress auxin response

edges.mechanism: Aux/IAA binds ARF7 (NPH4) forming repressive complex
edges.evidence_sentence: Aux/IAA proteins interact with ARF proteins to repress auxin response

edges.mechanism: Aux/IAA binds ARF8 to repress activity
edges.evidence_sentence: Aux/IAA proteins interact with ARF proteins to repress auxin response

edges.mechanism: ARF6 directly activates SAUR19/SAUR63 and other cell elongation genes
edges.evidence_sentence: ARF6 promotes hypocotyl elongation; arf6 single mutants 80-90% of WT length

edges.mechanism: ARF7 (NPH4) activates auxin-responsive elongation genes; nph4 has open hooks and short hypocotyls
edges.evidence_sentence: nph4-1 dark-grown seedlings had partially open apical hooks and shorter hypocotyls

edges.mechanism: ARF8 redundantly activates elongation genes
edges.evidence_sentence: ARF6, NPH4/ARF7, and ARF8 together account for hypocotyl elongation response to auxin

edges.mechanism: ARF6 directly activates SAUR19 and SAUR63 transcription
edges.evidence_sentence: SAUR19 and SAUR63 are downstream targets of ARFs

edges.mechanism: ARF6 activates IAA19 transcription creating negative feedback
edges.evidence_sentence: ARF-deficient seedlings had decreased expression of auxin-induced genes IAA1, AXR2/IAA7, IAA19

edges.mechanism: SAUR19 binds and inhibits PP2C-D phosphatases that dephosphorylate H+-ATPases
edges.evidence_sentence: SAUR19 protein that inactivates a PP2C-family phosphatase leading to AHA activation

edges.mechanism: PP2C-D dephosphorylates penultimate Thr of AHA1, inactivating proton pump
edges.evidence_sentence: PP2C-D phosphatase dephosphorylates and inactivates plasma membrane H+-ATPases

edges.mechanism: SAUR19 indirectly activates AHA1 by inhibiting PP2C-D
edges.evidence_sentence: SAUR19 inactivates PP2C phosphatase enabling AHA phosphorylation

edges.mechanism: Plasma membrane H+-ATPase AHA1 acidifies apoplast for cell wall loosening; constitutive activation drives elongation
edges.evidence_sentence: auxin induces extrusion of protons into the apoplast, activating expansins

edges.mechanism: AHA2 plasma membrane proton pump promotes apoplastic acidification and elongation
edges.evidence_sentence: ost2-2D/aha2-5 line elongates in absence of auxin

edges.mechanism: Auxin triggers cell wall acidification and elongation via TIR1/AFB-IAA-ARF-SAUR-AHA cascade
edges.evidence_sentence: auxin-stimulated acidification and growth require TIR1/AFB-Aux/IAA

edges.mechanism: TAA1 catalyzes Trp to indole-3-pyruvic acid (IPA), first step of YUCCA pathway IAA biosynthesis
edges.evidence_sentence: TAA1 encodes Trp aminotransferase catalyzing Trp conversion to indole-3-pyruvic acid

edges.mechanism: YUC1 catalyzes IPA to IAA conversion (FMO step)
edges.evidence_sentence: yuc1D overexpression increases root IAA

edges.mechanism: YUC8 produces IAA, key for shade and warm-temperature elongation
edges.evidence_sentence: YUC8 expression well correlated with PIF7 activity

edges.mechanism: YUC9 synthesizes IAA in shade response
edges.evidence_sentence: yuc2589 quadruple mutant has shade response defect

edges.mechanism: PIF7 directly binds YUC8 promoter G-box to activate auxin biosynthesis in shade
edges.evidence_sentence: PIF7 binding to DNA was similar in 21FR and 30FR; YUC8 expression well correlated with PIF7 activity

edges.mechanism: PIF7 directly binds IAA19 promoter G-box
edges.evidence_sentence: PIF7 binding to G-boxes in promoters of auxin biosynthesis genes

edges.mechanism: PIF7 binds IAA29 promoter, upregulating expression 6-70 fold in shade
edges.evidence_sentence: PIF7 binds to IAA29 promoter; expression upregulated 6-70 fold in low R/FR conditions

edges.mechanism: Shade (low R:FR) triggers PIF7 dephosphorylation enabling nuclear accumulation
edges.evidence_sentence: shade light induces the de-phosphorylation and nuclear accumulation of PIF7

edges.mechanism: 14-3-3 proteins bind phosphorylated PIF7 (S139/S141) and retain it in cytoplasm
edges.evidence_sentence: 14-3-3 proteins bind phosphorylated PIF7 at S139/S141 sites; blocking interaction accelerates nuclear localization

edges.mechanism: Active CRY1 represses PIF7 activity in blue light
edges.evidence_sentence: PIF7 works downstream of both phyB and cry1

edges.mechanism: Auxin activates MKK5-MPK3/MPK6 cascade to phosphorylate GRF4, releasing BZR1 from cytoplasmic 14-3-3 sequestration
edges.evidence_sentence: PIC treatment promoted strong BZR1-YFP accumulation in nucleus similar to eBL

edges.mechanism: MPK3 phosphorylates GRF4 (14-3-3) at S248, triggering its proteasomal degradation
edges.evidence_sentence: MPK3/MPK6 phosphorylate GRF4 at serine-248 (S248) promoting degradation

edges.mechanism: MPK6 phosphorylates GRF4 for degradation
edges.evidence_sentence: MPK3/MPK6 phosphorylate GRF4 at serine-248 (S248) promoting degradation

edges.mechanism: MKK5 phosphorylates and activates MPK3
edges.evidence_sentence: auxin activates MKK5-MPK3/MPK6 signaling cascade

edges.mechanism: MKK5 phosphorylates and activates MPK6
edges.evidence_sentence: MKK5-MPK3/MPK6 signaling cascade

edges.mechanism: 14-3-3 protein GRF4 binds phosphorylated BZR1 and retains it in cytoplasm
edges.evidence_sentence: GRF4 (14-3-3 protein) retains phosphorylated BZR1 in cytoplasm

edges.mechanism: Ethylene binding to ETR1 receptor inactivates downstream CTR1 kinase
edges.evidence_sentence: ethylene receptors bind ethylene and inactivate CTR1

edges.mechanism: Unbound ETR1 keeps CTR1 active (inhibiting EIN2)
edges.evidence_sentence: active CTR1 in absence of ethylene

edges.mechanism: CTR1 phosphorylates EIN2 C-terminus, retaining it at ER, in absence of ethylene
edges.evidence_sentence: active CTR1 phosphorylates EIN2 to suppress signaling

edges.mechanism: Dephosphorylated EIN2 C-terminus translocates to nucleus and inhibits EBF1/2 mRNA translation, stabilizing EIN3
edges.evidence_sentence: Ethylene-induced stabilization of EIN3 requires EIN2

edges.mechanism: EIN2 stabilizes EIL1 by promoting EBF1/2 degradation
edges.evidence_sentence: Ethylene-induced stabilization of EIN3 and EIL1 requires EIN2

edges.mechanism: EBF1 F-box protein recruits EIN3 to SCF E3 ligase for proteasomal degradation
edges.evidence_sentence: EIN3 proteolysis is mediated by two F-box proteins, EBF1 and EBF2

edges.mechanism: EBF2 F-box recruits EIN3 for degradation
edges.evidence_sentence: EBF2 mediates EIN3 degradation

edges.mechanism: EBF1 also targets EIL1 for degradation
edges.evidence_sentence: EIL1 is subject to SCFEBF1/EBF2-mediated degradation

edges.mechanism: EBF2 targets EIL1
edges.evidence_sentence: EIL1 is subject to SCFEBF1/EBF2-mediated degradation

edges.mechanism: EIN3 binds EBF2 promoter and activates transcription, forming negative feedback
edges.evidence_sentence: EBF2 is transcriptionally induced by EIN3 that directly binds promoter of EBF2

edges.mechanism: EIN3 directly activates ERF1 transcription, restricting hypocotyl elongation
edges.evidence_sentence: EIN3 directly activates its target transcription factor ERF1, which restricts hypocotyl elongation

edges.mechanism: EIN3 activates PIF3 transcription in light, contributing to ethylene-induced apical hook
edges.evidence_sentence: ethylene induces the transcription of PIF3 through EIN3

edges.mechanism: EIN3 binds APD7 promoter (PP2C-D phosphatase), suppressing H+-ATPase activity
edges.evidence_sentence: EIN3 binds to the promoter of APD7 and induces its expression

edges.mechanism: APD7 PP2C-D phosphatase inactivates H+-ATPase to inhibit cell elongation
edges.evidence_sentence: induction of APD7 suppresses the H+-ATPases activities and inhibits cell elongation

edges.mechanism: Ethylene inhibits hypocotyl elongation in dark (triple response) via EIN3-ERF1; promotes elongation in light via auxin biosynthesis
edges.evidence_sentence: ACC treatment shortens hypocotyl in dark (triple response)

edges.mechanism: EIN3 represses dark-grown hypocotyl elongation as part of triple response
edges.evidence_sentence: EIN3 overexpression shows ethylene hypersensitivity (short hypocotyl in dark)

edges.mechanism: CTR1 inhibits ethylene signaling; ctr1 loss-of-function gives constitutive triple response (short hypocotyl in dark)
edges.evidence_sentence: ctr1-1 mutants display hypocotyls shorter than Col-0

edges.mechanism: EIN2 transmits ethylene signal; ein2 mutants are insensitive (longer in dark + ACC)
edges.evidence_sentence: ein2 mutant is ethylene insensitive (longer hypocotyls)

edges.mechanism: Warm temperature upregulates SDIR1 RING E3 ligase
edges.evidence_sentence: high temperature up-regulates the expression of SDIR1

edges.mechanism: SDIR1 ubiquitinates EBF1 for degradation, stabilizing EIN3 (cross-pathway)
edges.evidence_sentence: SDIR1 directly interacts and ubiquitinates both EBF1 and EBF2 for degradation

edges.mechanism: SDIR1 ubiquitinates EBF2
edges.evidence_sentence: SDIR1 ubiquitinates both EBF1 and EBF2 for degradation

edges.mechanism: ARF6 directly activates PRE family HLH growth factors
edges.evidence_sentence: BZR1, ARF6, and PIF4 directly activate members of PRE family

edges.mechanism: PIF4 activates PRE1
edges.evidence_sentence: PIF4 directly activates PRE family

edges.mechanism: PIF4 heterodimerizes with PIF7 enhancing thermomorphogenesis in warm light
edges.evidence_sentence: PIF7 activity depends on PIF4 in 30WL but not in 21FR or 30FR

edges.mechanism: PIF7 promotes hypocotyl elongation in shade and warm shade conditions via auxin biosynthesis
edges.evidence_sentence: pif7 mutant eliminated synergistic elongation at 30FR

edges.mechanism: Active phyB Pfr promotes PIF7 phosphorylation; shade (Pr) releases PIF7 dephosphorylation/nuclear accumulation
edges.evidence_sentence: Low R/FR converts phyB to its inactive form, relieving repression of PIF7

nph4-1 single mutants 80-90% of WT
arf8-3 single mutants 80-90% of WT
Plants deficient in all three ARFs were 20% to 30% of wild-type in darkness

shy2-2 stabilized IAA3 shows short hypocotyl
Hypocotyls of axr2/iaa7-7 axr3/iaa17-7 double mutant seedlings were longer than wild-type
tir1 afb quadruple mutants severely impaired in auxin response
tir1 mutant has reduced auxin response
afb5-5 leads to decreased sensitivity to picloram-induced hypocotyl elongation
auxinole largely inhibits auxin induced hypocotyl elongation
auxin (NAA) treatment promotes hypocotyl elongation
Picloram caused stronger elongation response at 30°C
NPA blocked growth in all four conditions
35S::GFP-SAUR19 stabilized SAUR shows auxin-independent elongation
ost2-2D/aha2-5 mutant line elongates in absence of auxin
mpk3-1 mutant showed shorter hypocotyl
mpk6-3 mutant exhibited shorter hypocotyl
grf4 mutant exhibited long hypocotyl phenotype
GRF4-OE lines had shorter hypocotyls
etr1-1 dominant gain-of-function is ethylene insensitive
ein2-5 mutant is ethylene insensitive (longer hypocotyl)
ein3-1 mutant has reduced ethylene response
ein3 eil1 double mutants exhibit completely ethylene-insensitive phenotypes
EIN3 overexpression hypersensitivity (short hypocotyl)
ctr1-1 mutants display hypocotyls shorter than Col-0
ebf1 ebf2 double mutant constitutively short with high EIN3
35S:EBF1 displays ethylene insensitivity
ACC treatment of WT seedlings induces triple response (short hypocotyl)
seedlings grown at 28°C with identical ethylene treatment exhibit almost no change in hypocotyl
severe shade-defective phenotype of pif7 mutants
PIF7(2A) transgenic lines show increased nuclear localization and augmented hypocotyl elongation
pif457 triple mutant strongest defect in shade thermomorphogenesis
yuc2589 strongly suppressed 21FR response
14-3-3 λκ double mutant shows faster PIF7 nuclear accumulation
